rule_name: Service Registry Key Read Access Request

text: when the event matches devicetype=12 AND "Event ID"=4663 AND (LOWER("Object Name") LIKE '%\system\%' AND LOWER("Object Name") LIKE '%controlset\services\%') AND "Rule Name" LIKE '%%%1538%' AQL filter query

_$.description: Converted from SIGMA Rule.  Original Author: Center for Threat Informed Defense (CTID) Summiting the Pyramid Team SIGMA Title: Service Registry Key Read Access Request SIGMA Description: Detects "read access" requests on the services registry key. Adversaries may execute their own malicious payloads by hijacking the Registry entries used by services. Adversaries may use flaws in the permissions for Registry keys related to services to redirect from the originally specified executable to one that they control, in order to launch their own code when a service starts.   False positives: - Likely from legitimate applications reading their key. Requires heavy tuning  This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
_$.name: Service Registry Key Read Access Request
